news.headline: Amazon Stock Eyes Breakout On Meta AI Chip Deal
news.summary: Amazon stock climbed early Friday toward a potential breakout following news it will provide its Graviton processors to Facebook parent Meta Platforms. Meta stock gained as well. The companies announced that Meta will use "tens of millions" of Amazon Web Services' Graviton CPUs (central processing units) to power its AI offerings, according to the announcement.

news.headline: Meta Agrees to Deploy Millions of Amazon AI Chips in Deal Worth Billions
news.summary: Facebook parent company Meta has agreed to a multi-year deal to use Amazon’s AI chips in a deal that AWS says is worth billions of dollars.

news.headline: Stock buybacks stay hot, while Big Tech beasts like Meta lay off workers en masse
news.summary: While the HR departments at Big Tech work overtime to craft exit packages and collect laptops from laid-off workers, companies also appear to be working overtime to repurchase stock.

news.headline: Ironclad Honored in Fast Company's Annual List of the World's Most Innovative Companies
news.summary: Ironclad, the AI contracting company accelerating business with every contract, is proud to have been named to Fast Company's prestigious list of the World's Most Innovative Companies of 2026, joining the ranks of Google, Nvidia, Adidas, and Walmart. This year's list shines a spotlight on businesses that are shaping industry and culture through their innovations.

news.headline: Tuya Smart Unveils Upgraded Hey Tuya and Expanded AI Capabilities for Hardware Innovation, Advancing AI Home, AI Robot, and AI Energy Ecosystems
news.summary: Tuya Smart (NYSE: TUYA, HKEX: 2391), a leading global AI cloud platform service provider, today unveiled its newly upgraded Hey Tuya at the 2026 TUYA Global Developer Summit. Tuya also presented its strategic focus on three core AI application ecosystems: AI Home, AI Robot, and AI Energy.

news.headline: UnitedHealthcare Champions Industry Effort to Standardize Prior Authorization Requirements
news.summary: April 24, 2026--UnitedHealthcare today announced more than half of its prior authorization volume will be included in an industrywide effort to standardize submission requirements for electronic prior authorization, and that number will advance to more than 70% by the end of 2026. This step is the next phase of the company’s ongoing efforts to modernize and simplify prior authorization for care providers and help patients more easily and quickly access safe, high‑quality, evidence‑based care, al

news.headline: Records Undone: Oil, Iran, and an AI Identity Crisis Sink Wall Street
How The Intuit (INTU) Story Is Shifting Around AI Risk And Valuation Resets
news.summary: Intuit's updated fair value estimate shifted from US$603.49 to US$594.11, a trim of about 1.6% that reflects a more cautious stance on the stock's pricing. This adjustment is consistent with a broader wave of reduced price targets, even as analysts debate how resilient QuickBooks and TurboTax are to AI risk and how much Intuit's own AI efforts could support future demand. As you read on, you will see how these cross currents are shaping the story around Intuit and what to watch as the...
